headline: Scotland Opens First Hospital Dedicated to Caring for Houseplants
meta_description: A unique business in Edinburgh offers healing care for indoor plants, complete with hydration support and pest treatment.
summary_short: Edinburgh's Hilda Houseplant Hospital provides specialized care for struggling houseplants, helping owners nurture their green companions back to health.
body:
In Edinburgh, Scotland, a novel business has opened its doors to an unlikely group of patients: houseplants. The Hilda Houseplant Hospital, founded by Rosanna Costello, treats indoor plants with the same dedication a traditional hospital offers its human patients. Plants arriving in distress receive tailored care, from hydration support systems to targeted pest management, depending on their individual needs.

The hospital reflects a growing recognition of how much people value their indoor greenery. Many plant owners struggle to keep their companions thriving, uncertain about watering schedules, light conditions, or when pests have taken hold. Costello's service bridges that knowledge gap, offering expert horticulture advice and hands-on treatment to restore plants to vigor.

For plant enthusiasts across Scotland, the hospital represents both a practical resource and a celebration of the relationship between people and the living things they nurture at home. It's a reminder that care and attention—whether for people, animals, or plants—can transform struggling life into flourishing growth.
